# Figure description: Figure_09.png

**Source**: `usgs_teniz_copper_bundle/descriptions/Figure_09.png` (PDF page 31)  
**Generated by**: anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5

---

## Figure Type
This is a statistical plot consisting of three cumulative frequency distribution graphs (A, B, and C) showing the distribution of ore tonnage, copper grade, and silver grade for sediment-hosted sandstone copper deposits.

## Coverage and Subject
The figure presents data from 70 sediment-hosted sandstone copper deposits (n=70 for graphs A and B) used as a grade and tonnage model. Graph C shows silver grade data for a smaller subset of 18 deposits (n=18). These deposits serve as analogs for assessing undiscovered copper potential in the Teniz Basin, northern Kazakhstan.

## Key Content
**Graph A (Ore tonnage):** Shows cumulative probability (0-1 on y-axis) versus ore tonnage in metric tons (logarithmic x-axis from 10k to 10B). Red circles represent individual deposit data points forming an S-shaped curve. A blue fitted lognormal distribution curve overlays the data. Key percentile values are marked: 1.9 Mt (10th percentile), 10 Mt (50th percentile), and 91 Mt (90th percentile).

**Graph B (Copper grade):** Displays cumulative probability versus copper grade in percent (x-axis from 0.1 to 10, logarithmic scale). Red data points show measured copper grades with blue lognormal fit. Marked percentiles are: 0.5% (10th), 1.2% (50th), and 2.6% (90th).

**Graph C (Silver grade):** Shows cumulative probability versus silver grade in grams per metric ton (logarithmic x-axis from 1 to 200). Data points (red circles) are more scattered and show lower probability values (maximum ~0.25), indicating silver is not present in all deposits. The 10th percentile value is marked at 46 g/t.

All three graphs show the characteristic S-curve pattern of cumulative frequency distributions, with blue fitted lognormal curves closely matching the empirical data points.

## Text and Labels
**Title:** "Figure 9. Cumulative frequency plots of ore tonnage (A), copper grade (B), and silver grade (C), for 70 sediment-hosted sandstone copper deposits. Percentiles (10th, 50th, and 90th) of measured values are labeled along the x-axis. Each red circle is a data point for a deposit; the blue curve is the calculated lognormal distribution based on the population parameters of the data. Values for tonnage and grade for the 90th, 50th, and 10th probability values of the distribution are illustrated by extending a horizontal line from the vertical axis to the data curve, then drawing a vertical line to the horizontal axis. The value for the point where the vertical line intersects the horizontal axis is labeled. k=thousands, M=millions, Mt=million metric tons, B=billions."

**Graph A labels:**
- Y-axis: "Probability"
- X-axis: "Ore, in metric tons"
- Notation: "n=70"
- Percentile markers: "1.9 Mt", "10 Mt", "91 Mt"

**Graph B labels:**
- Y-axis: "Probability"
- X-axis: "Copper grade, in percent"
- Notation: "n=70"
- Percentile markers: "0.5", "1.2", "2.6"

**Graph C labels:**
- Y-axis: "Probability"
- X-axis: "Silver grade, in grams per metric ton"
- Notation: "n=18"
- Percentile marker: "46"

**Page header:** "Estimate of the Number of Undiscovered Deposits    23"

## Research Value
This figure provides the fundamental grade and tonnage model used for the quantitative mineral resource assessment of the Teniz Basin. By showing the statistical distribution of ore tonnage, copper grades, and silver grades from 70 known sediment-hosted sandstone copper deposits worldwide, it establishes baseline parameters (particularly the 10th, 50th, and 90th percentiles) that can be applied probabilistically to estimate the size and grade characteristics of undiscovered deposits in the Teniz permissive tract. The good fit between empirical data and lognormal distributions validates the statistical approach used in the USGS three-part assessment methodology for this deposit type.
